project_goal: Add a minimal observable smoke harness that demonstrates planner output, contract-ready acceptance criteria, and command evidence tracking
non_goals: Full test coverage of all planner features
assumptions: Command evidence can be captured via shell redirection or structured logging
Smoke tests run in an isolated environment

features.title: Observable Smoke Harness
features.user_value: Demonstrates that the VCH Planner produces verifiable, contract-ready output with traceable evidence

acceptance_criteria.description: Smoke harness executes and produces JSON output conforming to FEATURE_SPEC schema
acceptance_criteria.oracle: Output JSON contains required fields: project_goal, features array with id/title/user_value, sprints array with id/goal

acceptance_criteria.description: All acceptance criteria within produced output have verification_type, oracle, and required_evidence fields
acceptance_criteria.oracle: Every AcceptanceCriterion object contains all three required fields with non-empty values

acceptance_criteria.description: Smoke harness command execution is captured with timestamp and exit code
acceptance_criteria.oracle: Evidence log contains command, timestamp, exit_code, and stdout/stderr for each verification command

acceptance_criteria.description: Verification commands are documented and runnable
acceptance_criteria.oracle: All sprint verification_commands execute successfully with exit code 0

definition_of_done: Smoke test passes all acceptance criteria
Evidence log is generated in machine-readable format
No modification to production planner code

sprints.goal: Create minimal smoke harness that demonstrates planner output structure with contract-ready acceptance criteria and command evidence tracking
verification_commands: python -c "import json; spec=json.load(open('tests/smoke/output/feature_spec.json')); assert 'project_goal' in spec and 'features' in spec and 'sprints' in spec"

python -c "import json; spec=json.load(open('tests/smoke/output/feature_spec.json')); acs=[ac for f in spec['features'] for ac in f.get('acceptance_criteria',[])]; assert all(all(k in ac for k in ['verification_type','oracle','required_evidence']) for ac in acs)"
sprints.rollback_strategy: Remove created test files and restore any modified files via git checkout
